conventions.advisory: Conventions are advisory only — nothing in the store validates or special-cases the documented namespaces.
agent_first_contact_sequence: atm conventions — read this guide, including the code-of-conduct
atm label list --project <CODE> — read every label's description first to understand the project's vocabulary before exploring tasks
task list --project <CODE> --label <CODE>:context:agent — get agent directions for working in this project
task list --project <CODE> --label <CODE>:context:repository / :context:documentation — discover repository pointers and documentation
code_of_conduct: Read before you write. Run atm label list --project <CODE> and read every label's description before introducing any new label.
Default setup is the baseline. Prefer seeded labels; do not reinvent them under new names.
Invent only when nothing fits. Agents are free to self-organize, but a human reviewing the Labels tab should understand why a new label exists.
State the intention in the label description. A label with no description is a flag for human review.
One label, one meaning. If intent diverges from an existing label's description, create a new label with a distinct name.
Humans reconcile. The Labels tab is the human's review surface for overlapping, contradictory, or undescribed labels.
first_time_human_sequence: create the project (Add in the Projects tab); project create auto-seeds the 17 default labels with descriptions
create seed index tasks (context:agent, context:repository, context:documentation) and initial work tasks, labeling as you go

namespaces.examples: open, todo, in-progress, done, blocked, review
namespaces.purpose: workflow states — labels only, no state machine
the labeled task contains documentation about the project
the labeled task contains a pointer to a code repository
agent direction when navigating the project
something on this task should be reviewed, updated, or altered
index task pointing at a repo — created on demand, not seeded
index task pointing at a doc/resource — created on demand, not seeded
who's working on what — last-writer-wins, no conflict detection
task relationships via labels — created on demand, not seeded

seeded_labels.description: workflow state: open; task is not started or is being considered
workflow state: todo; task is queued for work
workflow state: in-progress; someone is actively working on this
workflow state: done; task is complete
workflow state: blocked; task cannot proceed pending something else
workflow state: review; task is awaiting review/approval
task categorization: bug; a defect to fix
task categorization: feature; new functionality to add
task categorization: task; general work item
task categorization: chore; maintenance, refactoring, tooling
the labeled task contains documentation about the project
the labeled task contains a pointer to a code repository
agent direction when navigating the project; read these to understand how to work in this project
something on this task should be reviewed, updated, or altered
